title: Core Concepts of Algorithmic Game Theory
description: A flowchart illustrating the foundational components and interactions that define Algorithmic Game Theory, bridging computer science and economics.
relationships: Game Theory provides models of strategic interaction (e.g., games, players, strategies)
Computer Science provides tools to analyze computational aspects (e.g., complexity, efficient algorithms)
Mechanism Design creates rules for desirable outcomes in strategic settings
Price of Anarchy measures the inefficiency of selfish behavior versus a central authority
Computational Equilibrium focuses on the complexity of finding stable solutions (e.g., Nash Equilibria)
